channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Sacramento Kings
channel.description: Sacramento Kings trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: Joel Embiid for Domantas Sabonis swap mentioned as longshot scenario by rival teams
item.description: One longshot scenario that does get mentioned by rival team strategists is an Embiid-for-Domantas Sabonis swap with Sacramento … but even longshot might be underselling it. Both former All-Star big men could probably use a fresh start, but there's just no getting around the fact that Embiid, at 32, seems incapable of holding up for a deep playoff run. Which certainly shortens the list of potential suitors.

item.title: “I feel like anybody who has any type of success, as …
item.description: “Who in the league do you think takes the most disrespect to their legacy right now?” DeMar DeRozan: “I feel like anybody who has any type of success, as soon as you fail and don’t win a championship or something, it’s so easy to discredit players these days because everybody is so opinionated. I remember growing up, the only time you heard something about a player was when you picked up the paper the next day. You know what I mean? Nowadays, you got people on Twitter thinking they’re journalist

item.title: DeMar DeRozan on Gregg Popovich: He tricked me into becoming a playmaker and it opened up the game for me
item.description: DeMar DeRozan: I think I didn’t realize it when I got to San Antonio. My mind was always like, ‘I got to shoot it thirty, thirty-five times. I’m getting after it.’ You know what I mean? That was always my mindset. When I got with Pop, Pop taught me how to use my teammates to make it easier for me later in the game. Utilizing them, playing a certain type of way, breaking down the game, understanding the game, sharing the ball — that opened up the game for me super easy. You know what I mean? And

item.title: Keegan Murray undergoes ankle surgery
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Kings say forward Keegan Murray underwent an arthroscopic procedure to remove loose bodies in his left ankle Tuesday. The procedure was performed by Dr. David Porter in Carmel, Ind. Murray will continue his rehabilitation process and will be reevaluated in six to eight weeks.

item.title: Bulls add Acie Law IV to front office
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Chicago Bulls will hire Acie Law IV as VP of Player Personnel. Law was Director of Player Personnel for the Brooklyn Nets. He’s been Director of Amateur Scouting for the OKC Thunder and won a championship with them in 2025. Prior, he was a Sacramento Kings scout.

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Kings interested in Mitchell Robinson?
item.description: Still, sources around the league have pegged the Sacramento Kings as a threat to pry away Robinson in free agency. Scott Perry, the GM of the Kings, has long been a fan of Robinson after drafting him the 2018 second round as a member of New York’s front office. It was probably Perry’s best move in a tenure of blunders before Rose arrived. Perry’s Kings have no cap space but could maneuver the roster to open a competitive offer for Robinson, who has mostly been underpaid throughout his career ($6

item.title: Kings targeting Darius Acuff?
item.description: Sources around the league widely believe the Kings are targeting Acuff with this pick. There is a lot connecting them. Kings general manager Scott Perry and assistant general manager B.J. Armstrong are both born and raised in Detroit. So is Acuff. Perry coached Acuff’s father at Eastern Kentucky in the late 1990s.

item.title: Sources around the league widely believe the Kings are …
item.description: Sources around the league widely believe the Kings are targeting Acuff with this pick. There is a lot connecting them. Kings general manager Scott Perry and assistant general manager B.J. Armstrong are both born and raised in Detroit. So is Acuff. Perry coached Acuff’s father at Eastern Kentucky in the late 1990s. Maybe it really is that simple. There’s also the obvious fact that he fits Sacramento’s needs.

item.title: 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team:
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team: Ace Bailey, @utahjazz Jeremiah Fears, @PelicansNBA Collin Murray-Boyles, @Raptors Derik Queen, @PelicansNBA Maxime Raynaud, @SacramentoKings

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander recorded his 27th career …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: SGA passes Russ 👏 In tonight's Game 2 win, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander recorded his 27th career postseason game with 30+ PTS for the Thunder, passing Russell Westbrook (26) for the second-most such postseason games in franchise history. Only Kevin Durant (39) has more.

item.title: Kings will host their first group of six draft …
item.description: James Ham: Hearing the Kings will host their first group of six draft prospects on Friday: Emanuel Sharp (Houston), SG; Isaac McKneely (Louisville), SG; Jaden Henley (Grand Canyon), SF; Keyshawn Hall (Auburn), PF; Tre Carroll (Xavier), PF; Maliq Brown (Duke), Center

item.title: The star-studded training camp squad is highlighted by …
item.description: Head coach Frederic Fauthoux has named a 16-player roster for the senior national team of France heading into the upcoming July window of the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 European Qualifiers, as revealed by the French Federation of Basketball on Friday. The star-studded training camp squad is highlighted by the returns of veterans Rudy Gobert and Evan Fournier, alongside a strong NBA contingent featuring Bilal Coulibaly, Zaccharie Risacher, Alex Sarr, Guerschon Yabusele, and newcomer Maxime Ra

item.title: Kings enamored with Darius Acuff Jr.
item.description: The Sacramento Kings are enamored with Arkansas star guard Darius Acuff Jr., according to league sources, but will he still be on the board by the seventh overall pick?

item.title: Here he is in his first postseason with the Spurs, …
item.description: Yet for all of the angst that De'Aaron Fox is feeling at the moment, none of it appears to be of the big-picture variety. Never mind the noise that has surrounded him of late. Here he is in his first postseason with the Spurs, having forced his way to San Antonio last season as part of his Sacramento Kings exit plan, and there’s already league-wide chatter about how his time here might be cut short. The assertion, shared by rival executives and media folks alike, is that rookie guard Dylan Harp

item.title: Execs think De'Aaron Fox's time in San Antonio could end sooner than expected
item.description: Here he is in his first postseason with the Spurs, having forced his way to San Antonio last season as part of his Sacramento Kings exit plan, and there’s already league-wide chatter about how his time here might be cut short. The assertion, shared by rival executives and media folks alike, is that rookie guard Dylan Harper (the No. 2 pick in last year’s draft) is simply too good to come off the bench for long and will eventually need to start alongside second-year guard Stephon Castle (the No.

item.title: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head …
item.description: Peter Hooley: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head coach, Dave Joerger, has been interviewed by Melbourne United about their vacant head coaching position Joerger has a strong relationship with Joe Ingles and is currently assistant at the Milwaukee Bucks.

item.title: Dave Joerger in talks to coach Australian team
item.description: Melbourne United are the last team in the League looking for a head coach, and Hools is Hearing that a very prominent and well-respected NBA name is at the top of the list. Current Milwaukee Bucks assistant Dave Joerger has been interviewed by the club recently about potentially taking over after Dean Vickerman’s departure. The 52-year-old has previously been Head Coach of the Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings, before being added to the Bucks staff two years ago.

item.title: Baba Miller was sick and could not participate in the …
item.description: James Ham: Baba Miller was sick and could not participate in the workout today in Sacramento.

item.title: DeRozan: LA Lakers basketball? Jefferson: Yeah, LA. …
item.description: Richard Jefferson: Start, bench, cut. LA basketball royalty. Kobe Bryant, Magic Johnson, LeBron James. DeMar DeRozan: LA Lakers basketball? Jefferson: Yeah, LA. Yeah. DeRozan: Start Magic, bench Kobe, cut LeBron. Jefferson: Yeah, that's fair. LeBron's been here for like 12 years. We don't need to make it a thing.
